title: Understanding the Forces
text: To find the magnitude of the horizontal force F, consider the forces acting on the girl. The tension in the rope and the gravitational force are also involved.

title: Components of Tension
text: The tension in the rope can be broken down into two components: one vertical and one horizontal. The vertical component balances the gravitational force, and the horizontal component is equal to the force F.

title: Using Trigonometry
text: Use trigonometric functions to find the components of the tension. The angle given is $$30$$ degrees with respect to the vertical.

title: Calculate the Vertical Component of Tension
text: The vertical component of tension T is equal to the gravitational force. Use the equation $$T cos\left(30\right)=m g$$, where $$m$$ is the mass and g is the acceleration due to gravity.

title: Calculate the Horizontal Component of Tension
text: The horizontal component of tension T is equal to the force F. Use the equation $$T sin\left(30\right)=F$$.

title: Solve for F
text: Substitute the value of T from the vertical component equation into the horizontal component equation to solve for F.
